titleStmt.title: SII 20.166: original edition by G. S. Gai
No. 166. (B.K. No. 63 of 1937-38.) RŪGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Right side of a stone standing close to the front wall of the Hanumān temple.

summary.p: This damaged record is dated in the second year of Mallugidēva, Durmukhi, Monday, Saṁkrānti; the other details are not given. It is engraved in characters of about the 12th century. During the reign-period of Mallugi, the cyclic year corresponded to A.D. 1176.

The inscription registers a gift of land by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvarara Chāmuṇḍarasa who was governing Taddavāḍi-1000 District, for the benefit of a temple (name lost) of the agrahāra Āneya Rūvige in Mūvattāṛuṁbāḍa. The gift was entrusted into the hands of Sarbbēśvaradēva. It also records other gifts by prabhu Brahmadēva and others (details lost).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti śrī samadhigata paṁcama
hāśabda maṁḍaḷēśvara
kōḷāḷapura
ra jīmūta
worn out
śvara viṣamahayārūḍharē
vanta vairibaḷakrilauṁta śrī
kātyāyanīdēvīlabu
dhavaraprasāda
maṁneyavallabha bhayalō
bhadurllabha rāditya
śrī gōgēśvaradēvara pādā
rādhaka kuśalāgrabuddhi
nerevoḍe gaṁḍa Āridhīramaṁ
ḍaḷikanivāraṇaṁ nāmādi samasta
prasastisahitaṁ śrīmahā
maṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ bhujabaḷa
cāmuṁḍarasaru . © ta
ddavāḍi sāsirada maṁneya bhuja
baḷa cāmuṁḍarasaru śrīma
tu bhujabaḷamalla mallugi
dēvavarṣada 2 ḍeneya durmmukhi
saṁvatsarada sōmavāra viti
pāta saṁkrāṁtiya parvvadaṁ
du jīrṇnōddhāranimittavā
gi mūvattāṟuṁ bāḍa
danādiagrahāra Āneya rū
vigeya śrīma śvaradē
vara svasti yamaniya
māsana prāṇāyāma pra
tyāhāradhāraṇādhyāna
samāḷaṁkrita
hārādisaṁpaṁnaru
mappa sarbbēśvaradēvaraṁ dhārā
pūrbbakaṁ sarbbanama
syaṁ māḍi biṭṭa bhūmi
maṭhageya saṭṭuga
yyaṁ paḍuvalu mukhavāgi
teṁkalu haḷḷa sīme baḍaga
lu moraḍi mēreyāgi mā
ṇikēśvarada biloḷu mattaru
Alliya prabhu
rasa prabhu brahmadēva
liga hoḷalu maṭha
ṭu hiṭṭu prabhu mukhya
biṭṭaru . ©
Digital edition of SII 20.166 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
